name: Flour Tortillas
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix flour and salt. Cut in lard until particles are the size of fine crumbs.
2. Sprinkle in water, one tablespoon at a time, until all flour is moistened and dough almost cleans the sides of the bowl.
3. Gather dough into a ball. Divide into 12 equal parts for 6-inch tortillas, 8 equal parts for 8-inch tortillas, or 6 equal parts for 10-inch tortillas.
4. Shape each part into a ball. Brush lightly with lard. Cover and let rest 20 minutes.
5. For each tortilla, roll one ball on a floured surface into a 6-inch, 8-inch, or 10-inch circle.
6. Heat ungreased griddle or skillet over medium-high heat until hot.
7. Cook tortilla until dry around the edge and blisters appear on the surface, about 2 minutes.
8. Turn and cook the other side until dry, about 1 minute.
9. Stack tortillas, placing waxed paper between each. Cover with damp towel.
Makes 12 6-inch, 8 8-inch or 6 10-inch tortillas.
